How much rent can you afford?

Use this tool to calculate the maximum monthly rent you can afford based on your hourly wage. This assumes a 40-hour work week and 160 working hours per month, with the standard rule that rent should not exceed one-third of your monthly income.

You can afford:
$0.00 per month in rent.

Formula used: (Hourly Wage × 160 hours/month) / 3. This calculation assumes you work 160 hours per month (40 hours/week) and follows the standard guideline that rent should not exceed one-third of your monthly income.
